slides.title: Daily Life & Communication
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 1: Daily Life & Communication. Welcome to Year 4 English! In this unit you will explore daily life and communication — the things people do every day and the ways we share ideas with one another. You will read about routines, letters and mail, working hard, and being fair. You will learn how people communicate in different languages, and why steady effort helps us gain new skills. By the end, you will be able to interview someone about their daily work, write clearly about your own routine, and use new grammar to make your sentences stronger — just like Sophia does in this unit's story. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Talk and write about your daily routine and the things you do every day
Explain why communication — letters, mail, and language — matters to a community
Use verb patterns correctly (for example, “She agrees to send the mail”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to talk and write about your daily routine and the things you do every day. you will also explain why communication — letters, mail, and language — matters to a community. you will also use verb patterns correctly (for example, “she agrees to send the mail”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: mail, language, citizen, agree, continue, gain. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Day at the Posthouse and My Daily Routine. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Verb Patterns
bullets: A verb pattern is the way we join a verb to the word that comes after it. Some verbs are followed by to + another verb, and some are followed by a verb...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Verb Patterns. A verb pattern is the way we join a verb to the word that comes after it. Some verbs are followed by to + another verb, and some are followed by a verb ending in -ing. Getting the pattern right makes your sentences clear. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe Your Daily Routine. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
